published_prior_art.cite: LuedtkeSofryginVanderLaanCarone2017 — longitudinal g-formula and sequential-regression/SDR formulation.
Standard bounded-integral consequence of L1-valued Hölder continuity of a transition kernel.

repo_prior_art.cite: No in-catalogue Stat entry studies a two-period transition-composed g-formula RMSE frontier; closest entries are stat_dose_response_minimax and stat_policy_regret_margin_overlap.

published_prior_art.cite: BonviniKennedyKeele2025 — two-time-point g-formula HOIF estimators and minimax-optimal rates in some anisotropic/additive structured models; separation from the proposed class remains to be established.
RobinsLiMukherjeeTchetgenVanderVaart2017 develops higher-order estimating equations for the missing-response model, but not this recursively fitted two-period pseudo-outcome architecture.
RobinsTchetgenLiVanderVaart2009 — two-convex-mixture lower-bound device; the proposed normalized transition-mediated pairing is distinct.
RobinsLiMukherjeeTchetgenVanderVaart2017 — higher-order missing-response benchmark after removal of the first transition channel.

novelty_flags.label: oeq:frontier / related_work
novelty_flags.one_line: The closest omitted comparator is RobinsLiTchetgenVanderVaart2016TR (arXiv:1601.05820), which explicitly says its monotone-missingness HOIF results apply to time-dependent treatment with time-varying confounding; contrast its assumptions and rate machinery with the proposed transition-L1-Hölder class.

soundness_flags.label: def:direct-sequential-hoif-handle / \(\widehat Z_2\)
soundness_flags.one_line: The declared \([-1/c,1/c]\) range for \(\widehat Z_2\) is false under the displayed formula because \(\widehat m_2\) is unbounded; clip \(\widehat m_2\) and give the resulting valid bound, or correct the object and all kernel/remainder controls that use it.

soundness_flags.label: ass:state-sufficiency-outcome
soundness_flags.one_line: Exact outcome-state sufficiency is load-bearing, but the generic physiologic-challenge description does not identify a real protocol or validation showing that the finite \(H_2\) removes all earlier-history dependence of the terminal outcome.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: oeq:frontier and its supporting OEQs
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: The claims are conditional on fixed smoothness indices, strict overlap, iid sampling, an exactly state-sufficient two-period protocol, and a fixed horizon.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: Any rate, elbow, estimator, and converse apply only to the stated two-period Markov observed-law class; adaptation, approximate state sufficiency, and longer horizons are excluded.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Add RobinsLiTchetgenVanderVaart2016TR and state exactly which component of its time-dependent-treatment HOIF construction fails to cover the incoming-state L1-Hölder transition class or the proposed two-channel frontier.

Make the direct handle internally bounded: specify a clipped \(\widehat m_2\), the exact bound for \(\widehat Z_2\), and use that version when defining the foldwise regression and degenerate correction kernels.

Anchor exact state sufficiency in one named two-decision protocol, define how \(H_2\) retains relevant history, and provide empirical or design-based validation evidence for the exclusion of omitted-history outcome dependence.
Give separate amplitude/resolution schedules for the \(r_1\) and \(r_2\) mixture branches and verify normalization, Hölder radii, target separation, and bounded product-law Hellinger distance.

recommended_next_step: Repair the pseudo-outcome definition, ground the exact state-sufficiency condition, and add the omitted time-dependent-treatment HOIF comparator before re-reviewing the matched frontier.
